{"society_power": 3.0, "state_power": 3.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Weak Qajar central rule dependent on tribes and clergy", "reasoning": "The Qajar state under Naser al-Din Shah had a nominal central court but limited administrative reach, weak revenue capacity, and dependence on provincial governors and tribal khans, keeping state power weak. Society was largely atomized, but Shi'a clergy (ulama), bazaar merchants, and tribal networks provided real if sporadic autonomous power outside state control."}
